name: Games for Freaks Vol. 1 — Virtues & High Blasphemies
author: Games for Freaks
description: Supplement containing the 7 Virtues of CAIN and the 6 High Blasphemies (Law, Null, Entwine, Strength, Veil, Accord).

prudence.name: Prudence
prudence.title: The Negotiator
prudence.desc:
Bearer of the powerful Accord blasphemy, Prudence is a friendly and charismatic Virtue with a very busy schedule. In addition to being a powerful fighter, Prudence plays a vital role both in CAIN's internal structure and in its relationship with mundane governments due to her unique ability to impose agreements. She has multiple cover identities, speaks several languages, and generally spends very little time 'on ice,' as CAIN deploys her on long-range, long-duration missions.

When appearing in public, she is always exceptionally well-dressed and articulate. As CAIN's frontline emissary to human governments, her identity has been the subject of speculation among online conspiracy theorists, something the organization has vehemently tried to suppress.

strictures: Ignore for one roll by taking 1d3 nonlethal stress
You must honor all agreements and promises.
Roll 0d when choosing violence over negotiation.

abilities.0: When you shake the hand of a willing human in an agreement or promise, if anyone breaks it, that person suffers instant death. This applies to you, and both you and your target are aware of the effects.
abilities.I: Gain the Accord blasphemy. You can use it once a mission.
abilities.II: Your rank 0 ability now applies to exorcists and sins.
abilities.III: You can use Accord one more time during a mission, but if you do, the Admin chooses the game.

hope.name: Hope
hope.title: The Dreamer
hope.desc:
Hope is among the most valuable assets CAIN possesses. Typically blessed with strong precognitive and telepathic abilities, her capacity to bear the high blasphemy Veil has been a cornerstone of the organization's activity for hundreds of years and allowed CAIN to operate in secret even in the most terrible circumstances.

Unlike most other blasphemies, Veil's full power can be imbued and passed on to other bearers, chosen from a pool of exorcists of extreme mental and cognitive strength. There has been a long lineage of Hopes (the current one is the forty-first) since the inception of the organization, and they are typically retired* when the stress of Veil proves too much to bear.

Due to her important role in the organization, Hope is never permitted to leave the SERAPH orbital station except in extreme circumstances, or once a year at Christmas.

*See CASTLE doctrine note C0447.

strictures: Ignore for one roll by taking 1d3 nonlethal stress
You cannot take actions that would be loud or attract attention.

abilities.0: Once per mission, you can reroll any action made in stealth or to avoid being noticed, taking the second result as final.
abilities.I: Gain the Veil blasphemy. You can use it once a mission.
abilities.II: Mundane humans always forget you were there if you stay out of sight for 77 seconds or more. You cannot turn this ability off.
abilities.III: You can use Veil to erase memory for longer periods. See the table on the following page. When used at full capacity, Veil can erase and reshape the memory of thousands, if not hundreds of thousands of people, for days on end. This use typically kills Hope.

fortitude.name: Fortitude
fortitude.title: The Disaster
fortitude.desc:
Considered a 'weapon reserved for calamity-level eliminations,' and one of the only exorcists in history considered close to exceeding category 7 in her abilities, Fortitude is kept on a strict cycle of inactivity and containment. An extremely volatile and potent fighter, her ability to execute sins is matched only by her thirst for complete and unbridled destruction. Releasing Fortitude into a situation is frequently the equivalent of trying to crack a nut with an industrial-grade hydraulic press.

Fortitude's high blasphemy, Strength, allows for unparalleled physical abilities, but without proper training, its users can literally tear themselves apart. Her marked contempt for human life and unchecked misanthropy have made her relatively unpopular in the organization's upper echelons, and she has relatively few admirers, even among the most pessimistic junior exorcists.

There is a special division of CASTLE's weapons division called the 'Break Division' dedicated to 'tempering' weapons for Fortitude to use, as without proper materials they cannot withstand the physical forces involved. The specialized research staff takes great pleasure in their work, and their discoveries have greatly advanced CAIN's armament.
favorite_food: Hot dog* *According to CASTLE addendum 3004, the subject can only consume nutritional paste

strictures: Ignore for one roll by taking 1d3 nonlethal stress
You cannot miss the opportunity to get into a fight
Roll 0d on actions that would require talk instead of action

abilities.0: You never roll 0d to inflict harm or violence (always roll at least 1d).
abilities.I: Any amount of harm you inflict is instantly fatal to humans.
abilities.II: You gain the Strength blasphemy. You can safely use it once a mission. If you use it a second time, when the scene ends, you shatter from within and suffer instant death.
abilities.III: You can safely use Strength a second time.

charity.name: Charity
charity.title: The Twins
charity.desc:
A widely feared Virtue, Charity immediately stands out among her peers due to her unique situation. Charity is functionally the same person occupying two bodies — or alternately, two separate people with exactly the same personality and physical appearance down to every strand of hair. They can even share emotions and can react or speak between bodies reflexively. This is because Charity's high blasphemy, Entwine, while extremely powerful, forces a fusion of its users so that over time they become perfect twins — an amalgam of the two, blended, physically and mentally. As Charity's abilities work at any distance, they are often deployed separately on different missions around the globe, though they prefer to work together.

It is common knowledge that there have been multiple generations of Charities over the years, since at least 1850, and none of their bodies are the original. When one dies, CAIN demands a replacement from a select group.

By bonding with Charity, you've placed yourself on this list. Congratulations.

strictures: Ignore for one roll by taking 1d3 nonlethal stress
When possible, you must engage in teamwork or preparation.
You roll 0d when trying to hide, use stealth, or avoid being noticed.

abilities.0: You can engage in telepathy with any exorcist with whom you have skin-to-skin contact.
abilities.II: At the start of a mission, you can choose an agenda ability from any other member of the group. For the rest of the mission, you can use this agenda ability as your own.
abilities.III: Powers that target 'yourself' can now target any exorcist you are Entwined with.

faith.name: Faith
faith.title: The Shy One
faith.desc:
Faith is not only an anomaly within CAIN, she is also an anomaly to the world at large. She was born in a village in Eastern Europe in 1672 and was forced at a young age to join a monastic order due to suspicion of being cursed or conspiring with the Devil. No matter how much Faith aged, she never seemed to pass her early twenties, remaining an image of health and peak conditioning, manifesting supernatural strength and constitution. After becoming a hermit some hundred years after her birth, she was discovered by an early CAIN, where it was determined she was the only purely Graceless and Sinless individual in the entire world.

Most humans manifest a small amount of Grace or Sin due to latent psychic trauma, but Faith is a 'complete void.' It is now theorized this is because she possesses an 'anti-blasphemy,' a blasphemy that absorbs all others. This has made her completely immune to psychic phenomena and also biologically immortal.

Faith has the lowest number of confirmed casualties among all virtues. Rumors say CAIN primarily uses her for capture and control missions of renegade binders, something she seems to be terribly effective at.

strictures: Ignore for one roll by taking 1d3 nonlethal stress
You cannot hurt anyone or anything.
Always roll 0d on actions that would be considered indelicate.

abilities.0: Once per mission, if you can eat sweets, you can relieve 1 sin.
abilities.I: You gain the Null blasphemy. You can use it once a mission.
abilities.II: Sin overflow only reduces your sin overflow limit by 1 instead of 2.
abilities.III: When you choose, your Null blasphemy becomes Heaven's Immaculate Challenge. This choice is irreversible.

justice.name: Justice
justice.title: The Executioner
justice.desc:
Justice is generally regarded as the most powerful exorcist in CAIN's current arsenal, a loner of few words given extreme leeway in engagement and unusual permission to roam. This is because Justice's high blasphemy, Law, will not allow them to disobey orders from a superior in any form, making them the perfect attack dog. They have the most recorded executions in CAIN history and a near-flawless fight record, standing as an object of awe amongst the regular rank and file.

For the most part, they seem to regard their position as an accepted reality and have come to embody their role as the headsman of CAIN. They are exceedingly efficient and the overwhelming nature of their abilities allows them to trivialize even the most tenebrous of opponents.

It is commonly theorized that Temerity has a special subdivision entirely committed to the contingency that Justice manages to circumvent their own blasphemy.

strictures: Ignore for one roll by taking 1d3 nonlethal stress
You cannot ignore orders from a superior.
Always roll 0d on any action that would break the law.

abilities.0: Gain an extra xp trigger: Did you uphold the dogma of CAIN?
abilities.I: Gain the Law blasphemy. You can use it once a mission.
abilities.II: Reduce sin by 1 after halving it. If you executed a sin rather than sparing it, reduce sin by 1 again. If you spared a sin or failed to execute it, increase sin by 1d3 after halving it.
abilities.III: You can choose two different effects for Law.

blasphemies.name: Law
blasphemies.desc:
A force so primitive it rewrites reality.
You don't bend the laws — you edit them.

powers.name: Law
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Adjacent, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining pulses — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
You establish a rule of physical reality that affects everything in a circular area around you, called a Court, with a radius equal to CAT.
Any changes occur instantaneously and persist within the area, but the area itself does not move.

This power can always eliminate, destroy, or completely alter mundane beings, objects, or structures.
Beings of strong will or supernatural nature, such as exorcists or sins, can partially resist its effects — but humans are always instantly affected.
If you leave the area or the scene ends, the effect ends.

To use Law, spend all your remaining psychic pulses (minimum 1) and complete the sentence:
"In the Court, _____ is/are _____"
You can only use words from the Law Terms list below.
Additional effects are at the Admin's discretion and can be improvised according to the fiction.

Then, choose one of the following effects:
• Grant up to three advantage dice (in total) to yourself or an ally while the Court is active. They can be distributed one at a time or all at once.
• Instantly kill all humans in the area or who enter it while the Court is active.
• Slash a talisman by rolling PSYCHE — +1 slash on success. Can be done upon activating Law and once more while it is active.
• Make something specific less difficult or less risky while the Court is active.

This power can easily affect the parameters of rolls within the area and can make rolls unnecessary for certain tasks.

LAW TERMS:
1. Fast   2. Slow   3. Glue   4. Stones   5. Paper   6. Fabric   7. Blood   8. Nails   9. Skin   10. Slings or bullets   11. Arrows   12. Blades   13. Solid   14. Liquid   15. Metal   16. Soft   17. Sharp   18. Heavy   19. Light   20. Earth   21. Fire   22. Water   23. Air   24. Forbidden   25. Attracted   26. Repelled

blasphemies.fato:
High Blasphemy — obtained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP limit nor reduce the sin overflow limit.

blasphemies.name: Null
blasphemies.desc:
The absolute negation of all psychic interference.
You don't resist the power — you simply don't exist to it.

powers.name: Null
powers.desc:
[Instant, Enchantment, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining pulses — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
For the remainder of the scene, you become completely immune to psychic phenomena.
Anything that causes physical damage — such as a Sin's physical attacks — can still affect you normally.
For example, you can be struck by a thrown piece of furniture or a bullet, but not by a purely psychic energy blast or mind control.

You cannot be affected by afflictions (though they remain on you, their abilities are simply suppressed during the scene).

In return, while this power is active, you cannot use or be affected by any blasphemy other than this one — including those of your allies — and any blasphemies actively affecting you are ended.

Unlike other powers, you cannot end this effect early.

powers.name: Heaven's Immaculate Challenge
powers.desc:
[Permanent]
This power has the same function as Null, except it is permanent. It has the following effects:

• You can no longer use or be affected by blasphemies.
• Your sin is zeroed out. You do not accumulate or use sin and cannot enter sin overflow for any reason.
• You can spend 1d3 stress instead of a psychic pulse to gain +1D on any action, using your powerful physical abilities.
• Combating Sins with mundane abilities is no longer difficult. In addition, your mundane physical abilities are equivalent to approximately ½ CAT in capacity.
• You gain +1 maximum wound.
• Your natural life expectancy extends by 10d10 years.

blasphemies.fato:
High Blasphemy — obtained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP limit nor reduce the sin overflow limit.

blasphemies.name: Entwine
blasphemies.desc:
You entwine your mind with another's in a loving embrace.
Distance does not exist between united souls.

powers.name: Entwine
powers.desc:
[Instant, 1 Mission | No pulse cost]
Choose another willing exorcist. You entwine your mind with theirs in a loving embrace.
For the rest of the mission, both of you gain the following effects:

• When one of you gains or loses stress, or spends/gains a psychic pulse, you can redirect it to either of you — regardless of distance.
Damage, stress, etc., appear supernaturally on each other's body.
Anything transferred this way cannot be reduced in any way.
• You can communicate telepathically at extreme distances.
You also reflexively feel any strong emotion the other feels — even involuntarily.

However:
• When one of you acquires an affliction or a hook, both acquire that affliction or hook.
• When one of you gains sin, both gain that amount of sin.
• If either of you suffers sin overflow or instant death, this blasphemy breaks for the rest of the mission. It cannot be ended any other way.

After the mission — even if the power broke before then:
• The physical appearance of both begins to change to reflect the other's (eye or hair color, age, gender, height, body or face shape, etc.).
Each chooses one characteristic to share with the other.
The process takes about three days and is unpleasant.

* Prolonged use of this power over time will lead to a physical and mental fusion described by Charity as the purest form of happiness. This has not been verified by TEMERITY.

blasphemies.fato:
High Blasphemy — obtained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP limit nor reduce the sin overflow limit.

blasphemies.name: Strength
blasphemies.desc:
A rare mutation of the Amplify blasphemy.
Without proper training, using this blasphemy will cause your internal organs to violently explode outward.

powers.name: Strength
powers.desc:
[Enchantment, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining pulses — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
Your mundane physical abilities are now equal to CAT and combating sins with mundane forces is no longer difficult.

• You can force any physical action up to CAT+2 by gaining 2d3 stress. This can inflict a wound.
• All weapons you use break after use, unless they have been specially tempered. You can temper any weapon between missions by spending 1 scrip per weapon.
• At the end of the scene, you suffer a wound and fall into a coma until your group rests. You recover at the start of the rest period and can rest normally.

blasphemies.fato:
High Blasphemy — obtained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP limit nor reduce the sin overflow limit.

blasphemies.name: Veil
blasphemies.desc:
You erase the memory of entire crowds in an instant.
They will never know what they lost.

powers.name: Veil
powers.desc:
[Instant, Long | Spend all remaining pulses — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
Instantly erase the memory of a mass of humans in a range determined by CAT.
You cannot choose who to target — you must target the maximum area and the greatest number of people possible.

They are completely senseless for exactly 11 seconds.
Then, they forget everything they saw and heard in the last 77 seconds (including the period of senselessness).
Their brain processes the damage and, for them, it is as if that time never passed — they are effectively 'reset' back 77 seconds.
They may be confused by the circumstances: for example, they may not be where they remember being.

powers.name: Mass Memory Rupture
powers.desc:
[Bond Rank III]
At bond rank III, you can force Veil for longer periods. If you do, you must pay the associated cost. Costs are cumulative.

Up to ten minutes: gain 1d3+1 stress.
Up to one hour: … and also gain 1d3+1 sin.
Up to ten hours: … and also permanently lose an important memory — such as the taste of something, your parents' names, a precious childhood memory.
Up to one day: … and also reduce the sin overflow limit by 2.
Up to ten days: … and you forget your own name. You cannot use agenda abilities nor gain XP this mission. You must change agendas before the next mission begins.
Total Reset: CAIN forgets you exist. You forget you exist. Your allies forget you exist. You become an empty shell, a walking corpse that no one remembers. You are not dead, but not truly alive either.
Create a new character.

blasphemies.fato:
High Blasphemy — obtained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP limit nor reduce the sin overflow limit.

blasphemies.name: Accord
blasphemies.desc: You impose an agreement on everyone around you, and reality bends to ensure the rules are followed.

powers.name: Accord
powers.desc:
[Transmutation, Short, 1 Scene | Spend all remaining pulses — min. 1. Sin cannot compensate.]
It is unclear why 'ACCORD' works based on children's games, but rumors say it relates to an inherent desire of humans for games, the 'homo ludens' theory.
You can spend all your remaining psychic pulses to instantly impose a game from the list below on yourself and all other humans, sins, or exorcists in a range determined by CAT. Breaking the game's rules causes excruciating pain, hemorrhage, and eventually death. While the rule applies:

• For you and allies, actions become more difficult when you must avoid breaking the rule with your actions
• If through your actions you or an ally intentionally break the rule, you suffer 2d3 stress, which cannot be reduced in any way and can inflict wounds as normal.

In return:
• If the target, through their actions, intentionally breaks a rule, they suffer 1d3 slashes on their execution talisman. Humans are instantly killed. They are aware of this effect.
• You can ready an ally for free, three times, while the rule is active, and without rolling. Describe how you are taking advantage of the rule. Any normal readying effects apply to these special readying actions, and you can perform them without counting as an action in a conflict scene.

You cannot end this effect early, and it lasts for the scene.

GAMES:
• The Floor is Lava (Don't Touch the Floor).
• King of the Hill (Must remain within a small area).
• Reverse Tag (Don't touch a sin, human, or exorcist).
• No Hands (Don't Use Your Hands).
• No Speaking (Don't speak or make loud noises).
• Marco Polo (Don't open your eyes. Targets must say 'Polo' when you say 'Marco' and cannot move beyond your short range).
• Red Light, Green Light (Those affected by the rule see a phantom red and green light and are forced to stop moving on red).

blasphemies.fato:
High Blasphemy — obtained through a bond with a Virtue.
Does not increase the XP limit nor reduce the sin overflow limit.
